structure.text: New / Save and set as in progress

structure.govukComponent: custom span.heading-tertiary nested inside the H1

structure.text: Choose from: / Yes / No — selecting Yes reveals a nested conditional Radios (Satisfactory / Not satisfactory)
Choose from: / Yes / No — selecting Yes reveals a nested conditional Radios (Satisfactory / Not satisfactory)

govukComponents.usage: Page H1 — combines the CHED reference + version (in a nested span.heading-tertiary) with the word 'Checks'
Groups each set of radios; legend rendered as 'Choose from:' (govuk-fieldset__legend). One fieldset per check plus one per nested conditional group.

All three checks are radio groups. Identity check and Physical check use govuk-radios--conditional: choosing 'Yes' reveals a nested Satisfactory/Not satisfactory radio group; the hidden conditional content carries govuk-radios__conditional--hidden until revealed.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (identy radios), trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 121 (documentaryCheck1); page object confirms locators at ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/decision/IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:50-79 (radioIdentityCheckYes #testExecuted1, radioPhysicalCheckYes #physicalCheckSelected1, and the nested Satisfactory/Not satisfactory locators)

govukComponents.usage: 'Save and return' and 'Save and continue' primary actions in main; 'Save and set as in progress' in the status banner above main.
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118; page object confirms btnSaveAndReturn + btnSaveAndContinue at IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:81-87

govukComponents.usage: 'Cancel' link (inside a p.govuk-body) returning to the notification hub
'Back' link near the top of the page (href=#, JS history back)
govukComponents.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (a11y tree ref e57)

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the CHED reference and version ('CHEDD.GB.2026.1526057 - V1') as a styled prefix inside the page H1, before the word 'Checks'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom, non-govuk class used to fabricate a caption. GOV.UK convention is a separate govuk-caption-xl element preceding the H1, not a nested span with a bespoke class.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Caption (govuk-caption-xl) placed above a plain govuk-heading-xl H1.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Bespoke CSS/JS hook classes attached to the form-group/fieldset wrappers, used to key the conditional-reveal behaviour and result targeting for each check.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Custom classes outside the govuk-frontend namespace acting as scripting/styling hooks. Not design-system components; they carry IPAFFS-specific conditional logic wiring.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-radios--conditional handles the reveal natively via data-aria-controls; no custom hook classes needed. Result state can be derived from the selected radio value.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 non-govuk class eval

fields.name: document-check-result
fields.label: Documentary check
fields.requiredEvidence: ConsignmentCheck.documentCheckResult carries @NotNull for the CED validation group (NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation) at ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/ConsignmentCheck.java:100-109. Additionally a class-level @DocumentCheckResult validator (NotificationDocumentCheckValidation group) applies, ConsignmentCheck.java:39-43. So the documentary check is mandatory server-side for CHED-D. Legacy mandatoriness — the rebuild may revisit whether a check can be saved blank as a draft. Note the rendered DOM carried required=false (client attribute absent); enforcement is server-side, not an HTML5 required attribute.
fields.evidence: trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 121 (#documentaryCheck1 value=1, name=document-check-result); trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (a11y tree e69-e79). Legend rendered as 'Choose from:'. Radios ids documentaryCheck1 (Satisfactory, value 1) / documentaryCheck2 (Not satisfactory). required attribute is false in the DOM but the field is functionally the inspector's decision. Legacy template confirms element name and CED branch: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-decision/service/src/views/bip/consignmentChecks.html:26-38 (Documentary check H2 + customRadioButtonGroup query='documentaryCheck', elementName='document-check-result'; the handler config maps CHEDPP/CED to query 'documentaryCheck', consignment_checks.js:86-97). Page object: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/page-objects/decision/DocumentaryCheckPage.ts:6-20 (radioSatisfactory / radioNotSatisfactory, #documentaryCheck1 / #documentaryCheck2 — note the page-object getters are misnamed 'checkbox...' but the controls are radios). Workflow ched-d-workflows.ts:390 drives Satisfactory (documentaryCheck1) on the acceptable path and :392 drives Not satisfactory (documentaryCheck2) on the reject path (permitChedDFromHub:473 / rejectChedDFromHub:496) — so both options are exercised even though only Satisfactory rendered in the corpus.

fields.name: identity-check-result-executed
fields.label: Identity check
fields.requiredEvidence: ConsignmentCheck.identityCheckDone (Boolean — the Yes/No 'was the identity check done' gate) carries @NotNull for the CED group (NotificationCedFieldValidation), ConsignmentCheck.java:114-119, message key ...consignmentcheck.identitycheck.not.null = 'Identity check' (ValidationMessages.properties:151). Mandatory server-side for CHED-D. Legacy mandatoriness — the rebuild may revisit draft-save behaviour.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (#identyCheck1-testExecuted1 checked; a11y tree e81-e102). Radios ids testExecuted1 (Yes, value=1) / testExecuted2 (No), name=identity-check-result-executed. Legend 'Choose from:'. Legacy template confirms the CED-only branch: consignmentChecks.html:114-138 renders the identity Yes/No gate via customRadioButtonGroup query='identyCheckExecuted', elementName='identity-check-result-executed' guarded by {{#eq type 'CED'}}, selecting Yes reveals the nested identity-check-result group (div id='identity-check-executed', driven by identityCheckConfigForDataTarget.CED.dataTargetMatchers). Page object: IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:50-52 radioIdentityCheckYes = #testExecuted1. Workflow always drives Yes (ched-d-workflows.ts:396, 475, 498) — the 'No' branch of the gate is never exercised in the CHED-D suite.

fields.name: identity-check-result-testExecuted1
fields.label: Identity check result
fields.requiredEvidence: The nested identity RESULT (Satisfactory / Not satisfactory) is NOT a plain @NotNull field for CED — ConsignmentCheck.identityCheckResult only carries @NotNull for the CVEDA group (ConsignmentCheck.java:123-130). For CED it is enforced by the class-level custom @IdentityCheckResult validator scoped to the CED group (NotificationCedFieldValidation), ConsignmentCheck.java:57-61, message key ...consignmentcheck.identitycheckresult.not.null = 'Complete identity check details' (ValidationMessages.properties:153). This is a CONDITIONAL requirement — the result is required only when the identity check was done (identity gate = Yes); the whole group is hidden until Yes is selected. Legacy mandatoriness; the rebuild may revisit.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (nested a11y group e91-e99; #identyCheck1-testExecuted1 = Satisfactory value=1, #identyCheck2-testExecuted1 = Not satisfactory, name=identity-check-result-testExecuted1). This nested group is inside the conditional content revealed when Yes is selected. Action 118 IS the Check on #identyCheck1-testExecuted1 (Satisfactory) — its after-snapshot shows checked=true — so 'Satisfactory' was observed selected in the corpus. Legacy template: consignmentChecks.html:128-135 renders this nested group via customRadioButtonGroup query='identyCheck', elementName='identity-check-result' inside the conditional div id='identity-check-executed'. NOTE the true form element name is 'identity-check-result' (shared with CVEDA); the DOM appends an id suffix per gate, hence the rendered id '#identyCheck1-testExecuted1' and the field-name observed here. Page object: IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:42-48 (locatorIdentityCheckSatisfactory #identyCheck1-testExecuted1 / locatorIdentityCheckNotSatisfactory #identyCheck2-testExecuted1). Workflow drives Satisfactory on the acceptable path (ched-d-workflows.ts:398, 476 — captured here) and Not satisfactory on the reject path (:400, 499 — inferred, not captured in these two traces).

fields.name: physical-check-done
fields.label: Physical check
fields.requiredEvidence: ConsignmentCheck.physicalCheckDone (Boolean — the Yes/No 'was the physical check done' gate) carries @NotNull for the CED group (NotificationCedFieldValidation), ConsignmentCheck.java:133-138, message key ...consignmentcheck.physicalcheck.not.null = 'Physical check' (ValidationMessages.properties:155). Mandatory server-side for CHED-D. Legacy mandatoriness; the rebuild may revisit draft-save behaviour.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 (a11y tree e103-e113; control eval physicalCheckSelected1=Yes / physicalCheckSelected2=No, name=physical-check-done). Legend 'Choose from:'. Legacy template: consignmentChecks.html:140-160 renders the physical Yes/No gate for CED via customRadioButtonGroup query='physicalCheckSelected', elementName='physical-check-done' guarded by {{#eq type 'CED'}}, selecting Yes reveals the nested physical-check-result group (div id='physical-check'). Page object: IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:77-79 radioPhysicalCheckYes = #physicalCheckSelected1. Workflow always drives Yes (ched-d-workflows.ts:402, 477, 500) — the 'No' branch is never exercised in the CHED-D suite.

fields.name: physical-check-result-physicalCheckSelected1
fields.label: Physical check result
fields.requiredEvidence: The nested physical RESULT (Satisfactory / Not satisfactory) is NOT a plain @NotNull field for CED — ConsignmentCheck.physicalCheckResult only carries @NotNull for the CVEDA group (ConsignmentCheck.java:140-147). For CED it is enforced by the class-level custom @PhysicalCheckResult validator scoped to the CED group (NotificationCedFieldValidation), ConsignmentCheck.java:62-66, message key ...consignmentcheck.physicalcheckresult.not.null = 'Physical check result' (ValidationMessages.properties:156). Conditional requirement — required only when the physical check was done (physical gate = Yes); the group is hidden until Yes. Legacy mandatoriness; the rebuild may revisit.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 118 control eval (#physicalCheck1-physicalCheckSelected1 = Satisfactory, #physicalCheck2-physicalCheckSelected1 = Not satisfactory, name=physical-check-result-physicalCheckSelected1). Nested conditional group, symmetric with the identity check; not visibly rendered in the a11y tree at action 118 because Physical=Yes was not selected at that snapshot, but present in the DOM control inventory (hidden conditional). Legacy template: consignmentChecks.html:153-158 renders this nested group via customRadioButtonGroup query='physicalCheck', elementName='physical-check-result' inside the conditional div id='physical-check'. NOTE the true form element name is 'physical-check-result'; the DOM appends a per-gate id suffix, hence '#physicalCheck1-physicalCheckSelected1' and the field-name observed here. Page object: IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage.ts:54-59 (locatorPhysicalCheckSatisfactory #physicalCheck1-physicalCheckSelected1 / locatorPhysicalCheckNotSatisfactory #physicalCheck2-physicalCheckSelected1). Workflow drives Satisfactory on the acceptable path (ched-d-workflows.ts:404, 478) and Not satisfactory on the reject path (:406, 501) — both nested options are exercised.

validationMessages.appliesWhen: documentCheckResult is null and the CED validation group runs (NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation) — i.e. no documentary-check outcome recorded on submit.
validationMessages.evidence: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-notification-microservice/service/src/main/resources/ValidationMessages.properties:150; annotation ConsignmentCheck.java:100-109. IPAFFS renders the raw property value ('Documentary check') as the error-summary link text / field label, not a full sentence. No error state was captured in the CHED-D traces, so this copy was never rendered in the corpus (gap closed from legacy).

validationMessages.appliesWhen: Class-level @DocumentCheckResult cross-field validator fires (NotificationDocumentCheckValidation group) — documentary-check result invalid/absent for a non-high-risk-EU consignment.
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:148; annotation ConsignmentCheck.java:39-43. Same rendered text as the not.null message; distinct validator/key.

validationMessages.appliesWhen: identityCheckDone (the Yes/No gate) is null and the CED validation group runs (NotificationCedFieldValidation) — i.e. the inspector did not answer whether the identity check was done.
Identity check gate = Yes but the nested Satisfactory/Not satisfactory result is missing — the CED-scoped @IdentityCheckResult validator requires the result once the check is recorded as done.
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:153; class-level annotation ConsignmentCheck.java:57-61 (groups = NotificationCedFieldValidation). Note the field-level @NotNull on identityCheckResult is CVEDA-only (ConsignmentCheck.java:123-130); for CED the requirement is the custom conditional validator.

validationMessages.appliesWhen: physicalCheckDone (the Yes/No gate) is null and the CED validation group runs (NotificationCedFieldValidation) — i.e. the inspector did not answer whether the physical check was done.
Physical check gate = Yes but the nested Satisfactory/Not satisfactory result is missing — the CED-scoped @PhysicalCheckResult validator requires the result once the check is recorded as done.
validationMessages.evidence: ValidationMessages.properties:156; class-level annotation ConsignmentCheck.java:62-66 (groups = NotificationCedFieldValidation). Field-level @NotNull on physicalCheckResult is CVEDA-only (ConsignmentCheck.java:140-147); for CED the requirement is the custom conditional validator.

openQuestions: PARTIALLY RESOLVED FROM LEGACY: no validation/error state was captured in the traces (both are error-free happy paths, and the CHED-D spec suite makes no assertion on error copy). The required-field error copy is now sourced from the IPAFFS ValidationMessages.properties + ConsignmentCheck.java @NotNull/custom-validator annotations — see the validationMessages array. CAVEAT: IPAFFS stores these as bare field-name labels ('Documentary check', 'Identity check', 'Physical check', 'Physical check result', 'Complete identity check details') that render as the error-summary link text, NOT as full GDS sentences like 'Select whether the documentary check was satisfactory'. The rebuild should author proper GDS error sentences; the legacy strings give the field identity and which fields are validated, not final copy. Confirming the exact rendered error sentence still needs an error-bearing /checks trace (none in this corpus).

The three checks (Documentary / Identity / Physical) render together on a single page, each as its own H2 section. Identity and Physical use a Yes/No gate that conditionally reveals a Satisfactory/Not satisfactory result; Documentary has no Yes/No gate and asks Satisfactory/Not satisfactory directly. Confirm whether the new app should preserve this three-in-one layout or split into one page per check. NOTE: the QA automation splits these into two page objects (DocumentaryCheckPage + IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage) but the workflow fills all three then clicks a single 'Save and return' (ched-d-workflows.ts:390-408), and the trace shows one /checks URL — corroborating a single rendered page, not a multi-page sequence.

The H1 embeds the CHED reference + version via a custom span.heading-tertiary rather than a govuk-caption-xl — the 'caption' captured here is derived from that span, not a true GOV.UK caption element.

'Save and set as in progress' sits in a status banner above main alongside a 'New' status label — this is a workflow-state control (draft/in-progress lifecycle) rather than a form field; confirm whether the rebuild needs the same explicit status transitions.

RESOLVED FROM LEGACY: the DOM reports required=false on every radio (client-side), but IPAFFS enforces mandatoriness server-side via the CED validation group. All three gate fields are @NotNull for CED — documentCheckResult (ConsignmentCheck.java:100-109, group NotificationCedOrCvedpFieldValidation), identityCheckDone (:114-119) and physicalCheckDone (:133-138) (group NotificationCedFieldValidation). The two nested RESULT fields are conditionally required for CED via class-level custom validators — @IdentityCheckResult (:57-61) and @PhysicalCheckResult (:62-66), both scoped to NotificationCedFieldValidation — required once the corresponding gate = Yes. required has been set to true on all five fields with requiredConfidence='legacy'. This is 'as the old system had it' — whether a check can be saved blank on a DRAFT (vs on final submit) is a policy the rebuild may revisit.

The IdentityAndPhysicalChecksPage page object is SHARED across CHED types and exposes several controls that do NOT apply to CHED-D: a 'Welfare check' Satisfactory/Not satisfactory group, animal-count number inputs (#number-of-animals-checked, #number-of-dead-animals, #number-of-unfit-animals, #number-of-birth-or-abortion) and a 'Full identity check' radio (radioFullIdentityCheck) — all CHED-A (live animals) only, confirmed by their use exclusively in ched-a-workflows.ts:722-1074 and their absence from ched-d-workflows.ts (which drives only radioIdentityCheckYes / radioPhysicalCheckYes). None of these are CHED-D requirements; listed here so the rebuild does not accidentally inherit them from the shared page.

The 'No' branch of both the Identity and Physical Yes/No gates is never exercised by the CHED-D suite (workflow always calls .check() on the Yes locators). Behaviour when a check is recorded as 'not done' (No) — whether the nested Satisfactory/Not satisfactory result is required/omitted, and what the resulting record state is — is uncovered by both traces and tests. Gap for a human.

Downstream, the copy-as-new test asserts a post-submit outcome banner on the decision hub: 'Your checks have been submitted {reference} - V1 Outcome: Acceptable for Internal Market' (tests/notification/ched-d/ched-d-copy-as-new.spec.ts:39, inferred). This is the confirmation after this page's checks are saved, not validation on the page itself — noted so the rebuild knows the checks feed an Acceptable/Not-acceptable outcome computed elsewhere.
